title: The Man Who Forgot His Wife
author: Barış Kısır
short_description: A man wakes from a coma remembering everything except the last five years — his wife, his child, and the crime he committed.
long_description:
Daniel Cross wakes from a three-month coma after a car accident with a peculiar form of amnesia: he remembers everything from birth to age thirty-two, but the last five years are gone — wiped clean. In those missing years, he married a woman named Sarah, became a father to a little girl named Emma, and committed a crime that left a woman dead.

As Detective Patricia Reeves closes in, Daniel must reconstruct the man he became, confront the truth about his marriage, and decide whether a man who cannot remember his sins can still be held accountable for them. His search leads him to a hidden storage unit filled with evidence, a business partner named Marcus Webb who wants him silenced, and the devastating realization that he was the architect of a fraud that destroyed dozens of families.

When Marcus threatens Sarah and Emma, Daniel agrees to wear a wire for the police. But the operation goes wrong, and Daniel is stabbed — waking in the hospital a second time with all his memories restored. Now he must face what he did, accept the consequences, and find a way to become the man his daughter deserves.
main_characters: Daniel Cross — A 37-year-old architect who loses five years of memory after a car accident
Sarah Cross — Daniel's wife, who knew about his secrets but stayed
Detective Patricia Reeves — The detective investigating Linda Pearce's death
Linda Pearce — The victim who confronted Daniel about his crimes
Marcus Webb — Daniel's business partner and the architect of the fraud scheme
